danger_signals.visual_form: Rusty Nail Inc. minions and agents
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile faction actively threatening the player's hometown; immediate threat to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Rusty Nail Inc. leadership and final antagonist
danger_signals.meaning: High-level threat; represents systemic danger to community

danger_signals.visual_form: Torn-down buildings, construction sites, urban decay
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard and narrative threat; visual representation of Rusty Nail Inc.'s destructive agenda

safety_signals.meaning: Tool of protection and agency; enables player to combat threats and defend community
Base location; represents what player is protecting; motivational anchor

safety_signals.visual_form: Sake bottle (Japan) or hot pepper (international); rice ball/cheese variants
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary enhancement and resource replenishment; safety mechanism for survival

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Corporate construction company branding (implied through narrative)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple minion types representing corporate enforcement agents
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Construction sites, demolition zones, torn-down buildings serving as level backdrops
Residential architecture; hometown setting represents allied territory

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Initial crisis - Harry's house destroyed by Rusty Nail Inc.
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game premise; visual context of demolition and urban destruction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player motivation to quest through stages defeating enemies and confronting corporate antagonist

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy presence throughout levels; construction company agents as stage obstacles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat-focused gameplay; player must defeat minions and advance toward Rusty Nail Inc. leadership

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Escalating enemy difficulty; stage-clear mechanics marking narrative advancement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stage start announcements (with regional voice sample variants) marking narrative beats; stage clear confirmations

environmental_storytelling.example: Levels set in partially destroyed buildings and active construction zones visually reinforce the threat of Rusty Nail Inc.'s development agenda
Consistent environmental locale communicates stakes; player defends familiar space from corporate encroachment
Game title and premise establish clear protagonist-antagonist relationship: Harry (construction worker/protector) vs. Rusty Nail Inc. (greedy developers)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hammer-wielding action; combat-focused movement through stages; progression toward boss confrontation
Patrol levels; attack player on contact; represent distributed corporate enforcement
End-stage encounter; represents concentrated threat requiring defeat to resolve narrative conflict

notes: Hammerin' Harry uses straightforward visual narrative: protagonist (carpenter with hammer) vs. antagonist (corrupt construction corporation). Regional localization affected visual presentation (title screen redesign, logo changes, power-up sprite modifications) but not core thematic communication. The game communicates political situation through environmental destruction and corporate enemy presence. Voice sample localization (stage start, life loss, stage clear announcements) reinforces regional cultural framing but doesn't alter world-state messaging. Danger is communicated through enemy encounters and destructive context; safety through weapon, power-ups, and narrative goal (defending hometown).
